```yaml
notes: |
  Excellent commission. The implementation change in `failEngine` is minimal and correct — a 3-line addition that cancels pending engines while preserving running/completed/failed ones. Tests are thorough: dedicated `downstream engine cancellation` describe block covers first-engine failure, mid-pipeline failure, running-engine preservation, and property checks (no completedAt, no error on cancelled engines). Existing failure tests were also augmented with cancellation assertions. The only minor gap is that error handling doesn't explicitly address edge cases like an engine already in `cancelled` status when `failEngine` is called, though the current `map` approach handles it implicitly. Documentation, types, and tests are all updated in lockstep, matching codebase conventions precisely.
```
